key_themes: Analyst price target upgrades and Outperform ratings
Stable regulated utility with predictable cash flows and dividends
Strong long-term EPS growth guidance (8-9% through 2028)
Infrastructure capex expansion and rate base growth
summary: Southern Company (SO) enjoys mildly positive sentiment as a defensive utility benefiting from surging power demand linked to AI data centers, electrification, and infrastructure investments. Analysts have issued multiple price target raises (Citi to $114, TD Cowen to $112, Evercore upgrade to Outperform), with consensus around $99 against the current price near $94.50; the stock is up modestly YTD but has pulled back slightly in mid-April ahead of Q1 2026 earnings on April 30. It is widely viewed as a stable hold with reliable dividends, consistent earnings growth projections, and regulatory strength, though broader market enthusiasm for high-growth tech has caused it to lag the S&P 500 recently.
sources: 12+ news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, Zacks, MarketBeat, TipRanks, PR Newswire), 20+ recent X posts (March-April 2026) mentioning $SO primarily in utility/defensive stock contexts
